what_it_does: A single-file interactive HTML/CSS/JS mobile mockup of a four-tab instrument (Board, Talk, Queue, Tune) for a proposed mobile information architecture. It renders a 390px phone frame with a warm-gold theme, uses a display:none view gate to show one tab at a time, and wires up tab switching, node selection, palette/inspector sheets, a show-me spotlight, push-to-talk orb states, and Tune configuration pills.
observations: Single self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript; no external JS libraries.
Four primary views gated by a bottom tab bar: Board (◈), Talk (◇), Queue (▤), Tune (⚙).
Views are mutually exclusive via CSS display:none / .active, explicitly avoiding stacked transform-sheet behavior.
Board shows state-coloured nodes (idle, ran, cached, stuck, failed, live) on a faint grid with connecting SVG wires.
Board includes a floating thumb cluster (Run, + FAB), palette short-sheet, inspector sheet, and a show-me spotlight overlay.
Talk centers a breathing orb with listening/thinking/rest states, a conversation thread, an offer card, F1 feedback chips, and a push-to-talk button.
Queue presents triaged cards with lane tints (decision, build-review, question) and approve/reject/comment actions plus a capture footer.

Tune consolidates live config slots, mode/sub-type pills, self-grow affordances, per-model knobs, R2 composition sliders, run-stats distribution, voice trial, self-change log, fleet status, and activity sparkline.
Uses CSS custom properties for a warm charcoal + gold palette and Google Fonts (Fraunces, IBM Plex Mono/Sans).
Many elements carry data-ui-ref attributes pointing to conceptual UI addresses (ui://...).
Boot logic supports ?tab= and ?show=1 query parameters to land directly on a view or the spotlight.
standouts: Explicit 'S1 fix' design rationale: one focused view at a time using display:none, not a held transform sheet.
Status-as-light vocabulary: node state read by colour rather than text labels.
Surfaced latent capabilities marked with ★ in Tune (self-grow, /api/knobs, R2 live setters, run-stats, voice trial, self-change log).
Push-to-talk as the dominant thumb target in Talk.
Offer card pattern in Talk for reversible self-modification (panel/component/node-type growth).
Show-me spotlight overlay that highlights real board elements step by step.
conventions: Follows a token-driven theme via CSS :root custom properties.
Uses semantic section elements per tab view.
Class naming is descriptive and block-scoped (e.g., .tune-scroll, .qcard, .insp-acts).
State encoded in data-st / data-state attributes and mirrored in CSS classes.
UI references encoded in data-ui-ref attributes rather than hrefs.
Inline script at the bottom with an IIFE for boot and event wiring.
concerns: Node positions are inline styles with pixel coordinates, making the mock layout brittle and hard to maintain.
No ARIA roles, labels, or focus management; likely inaccessible to screen readers and keyboard users.
All content is hardcoded mock data with no clear data layer or backend binding.
Global mouseup/touchend listeners for push-to-talk could fire unexpectedly outside the button.
External Google Fonts dependency may break offline or air-gapped viewing.
No error handling, tests, or build pipeline; intended as a prototype but could be mistaken for production UI.
notes: A polished, opinionated mobile-IA prototype that treats the phone as a calm, single-focus instrument and uses colour and motion as the primary status language.
questions: What is the exact definition of the S1 bug referenced in the header comment?
Are data-ui-ref attributes meant for analytics, automated testing, or future routing?
How would node positions be persisted or generated in the real implementation?
What does R2 refer to in the context-composition section?
Are the 16 node types in the palette actually auto-discovered, or are they a fixed catalog?
How is the self-change log revert operation implemented and validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—realize the proposed mobile IA with one focused view at a time and surface latent capabilities—matches the file's actual content and interactions.
intention_signals: The file appears to be making a design argument: replace a previous stacked-sheet mobile UI with a calmer, tab-gated instrument that exposes hidden system powers through deliberate visual affordances rather than buried menus.
novelty: The combination of a state-coloured node board, push-to-talk orb interface, triaged queue, and a Tune panel that advertises self-growing UI capabilities, all within one mobile prototype annotated with design rationale and latent-capability callouts.
contribution: A concrete interactive reference for a mobile IA direction, including a visual vocabulary (gold theme, state colours, sheet/spotlight patterns), a display:none view-gating pattern, and annotated examples of how latent capabilities could be surfaced in Board, Talk, Queue, and Tune contexts.
summary_for_embedding: Interactive mobile HTML mockup of a four-tab instrument (Board, Talk, Queue, Tune) using a warm-gold design language, display:none view gating, state-coloured nodes, a push-to-talk orb, and explicit surfacing of latent system capabilities.
intention_for_embedding: To realize and drive the proposed mobile information architecture before the real build, surfacing latent capabilities and fixing the prior sheet-stacking bug through a single-focus tab-gated layout.
